business.headline: Sensex jumps 1,000 points: Why is the stock market rising today?Today's gains on Dalal Street are being driven by easing West Asia tensions, softer crude prices and strong buying in banking stocks, as investors return to beaten-down large caps after Monday's sharp sell-off.
Sensex, Nifty jump as US delays Iran grid strike; IndiGo up 3%The S&P BSE Sensex was up 993.75 points to 73,690.14, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 319.20 points to 22,831.85 as of 9:29 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening today: Will stock markets crash again amid Iran war?Early signals show a positive start. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 22,878 at around 8:40 am, indicating that the Nifty 50 may open about 1.6% higher compared to Monday's close of 22,512.65.

$580 million bets before Trump's Iran remark. Coincidence or insider trading?Perfectly timed oil and equity trades worth $580 million before US President Donald Trump claimed "productive talks" with Iran and paused strikes, later denied by Tehran, are raising insider trading concerns.

IndiGo appoints former Air India Express CEO Aloke Singh as Strategy ChiefBetween December 3 and 5, 2025, IndiGo cancelled 2,507 flights and delayed 1,852 others, affecting over three lakh passengers nationwide. The disruptions were linked to challenges in implementing revised Flight Duty Time Limitation (FDTL) norms for pilots.

Wall Street rallies as Trump delays Iran strikesThe positive sentiment followed Trump's statement that the US would postpone planned strikes on Iranian power plants and energy infrastructure for five days.

Sebi eases trade rules for foreign investors amid rising market outflowsUntil now, investors had to settle each buy and sell trade separately. This meant higher funding needs and more complex transactions. With net settlement, they can offset buy and sell positions, reducing the amount of money required for each cycle.
Oil prices fall over 13% as Trump pauses Iran energy strikesOil prices jumped in the morning with Brent nearing $113, but later fell to around $100.34, down 6.2%, later in the day.

CoinDCX co-founders in custody over fraud claims, company blames fake operatorsThe case is based on a complaint filed by a 42-year-old insurance advisor. He alleged that he was cheated of Rs 71.6 lakh between August 2025 and March 2026.
Sensex ends 1,836 points lower, Nifty below 22,600; Titan down 7%The S&P BSE Sensex lost 1,836 points to close at 72,696.39, while the NSE Nifty50 tanked 601.85 points to end at 22,512.65.

Gold crashes Rs 10,000 in a day, silver down Rs 18,000: Should you buy now?Precious metals have taken a hit, with gold and silver prices falling sharply. While corrections are not unusual, investors are now wondering — is this a buying opportunity or a signal to stay on the sidelines?

Share market crash: Why are IT stocks shining when Dalal Street is in red?A look at Sensex stocks shows that IT names such as HCLTech, Tech Mahindra, and TCS were trading in the green, even as heavyweights across banking, auto, metal and consumption sectors saw sharp declines.

Your iPhone's India-made: Fareed Zakaria's US lecture highlights India pipping ChinaSmartphone shipments from India became its top export while it displaced China as the biggest supplier of iPhones to the US. The feat is being lauded yet again with analyst Fareed Zakaria highlighting it in his lecture. Where does India stand vis a vis China in the smartphones game?

Rupee in free fall: Will it hit Rs 95 against the US dollar?The currency weakened to around Rs 93.9 against the US dollar on Monday, marking its weakest level ever and bringing the Rs 94–95 range into focus.

IndiGo shares hit 52-week low, fall nearly 6%: What is dragging the stock today?IndiGo shares tanked nearly 6% to Rs 3,899.90, down Rs 248 or 5.98%. During the session, it touched a 52-week low of Rs 3,894.80.

China eyes Iranian oil deals after US grants limited sanctions reliefThe US has given a 30-day waiver, allowing some Iranian oil to be sold in order to prevent a sharp jump in global prices. While the relief is temporary, it has opened a small window for buyers, especially in Asia.

Safe haven no more? Why gold, silver prices are crashing despite warGold and silver price are falling even as global tensions rise, turning the usual safe-haven play on its head and catching investors off guard.

HDFC Bank share price falls 4%, hits 52-week low: Why is the stock down?Shares of HDFC Bank were down 3.39% to Rs 754 on the Bombay Stock Exchange at around 10:23 am, extending a sharp decline seen over the past few sessions.

Sensex crashed 1,800 points: 3 reasons why the stock market fell todayAs of 1:10 pm, the S&P BSE Sensex was down 1,923.51 points at 72,609.45, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 625.70 points to 22,488.80. Both indices were down around 2.6–2.7%, reflecting broad-based selling across sectors.

Rupee slides to record low, nears 94 as crude oil surge deepens pressureCrude prices have surged sharply in recent weeks as the conflict in West Asia intensifies. Prices are now hovering above $110 per barrel, a level that immediately hurts countries like India, which import over 80% of their oil needs.

entertainment.headline: Reacher star Alan Ritchson accused of assaulting neighbour over bike noise Actor Alan Ritchson is facing scrutiny after his neighbour alleged a violent altercation over dirt bike noise in Tennessee. His neighbour, Ronnie Taylor, claimed in a recent interview that he was punched by him multiple times.

Dhurandhar 2: Aditya Dhar gave Rakesh Bedi his best comic role ever, with a catch Rakesh Bedi's Jameel Jamali in Dhurandhar: The Revenge steals every frame with comic expressions and quirky dialogues, providing relief amid high-octane drama and gore. The actor's job, though, runs deeper in the script.

Superstar moment: Aditya Dhar reacts as Rajinikanth calls Dhurandhar 2 a must watch Filmmaker Aditya Dhar shared an emotional note after Rajinikanth called Dhurandhar 2 a must-watch. The action-thriller, starring Ranveer Singh, Arjun Rampal, R Madhavan, Rakesh Bedi and Sanjay Dutt among others, follows Hamza's mission against terrorism.

Is Dhurandhar 3 happening? Mukesh Chhabra shares update on Ranveer Singh's film Casting director Mukesh Chhabra put an end to Dhurandhar 3 rumours amid fan speculation. The rumours of a third instalment to the hit action thriller grew after a fake end-credit clip and director Aditya Dhar's post on end credits.

Dhurandhar 2: Ranveer Singh's best moments amid gore are ones that let him cry Ranveer Singh delivered a career-defining performance as an action hero in Dhurandhar: The Revenge. Four powerful scenes highlighted the versatile actor's unmatched emotional depth.

Valerie Perrine dies at 82, fans remember Superman actor's legacy Veteran actor Valerie Perrine has died at 82 after a long battle with Parkinson's disease. She was known for her roles in Lenny and the Superman films.

Hey Jude: Charlie Puth, wife Brooke Sansone welcome baby boy; share pics Singer-songwriter Charlie Puth and his wife, Brooke Sansone, welcomed their first child. The couple shared heartfelt glimpses of baby Jude on Instagram.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 5 crushes Pushpa 2 Hindi, Rs 800 crore worldwide Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge has entered the Rs 500 crore club domestically in five days, and has surpassed the Rs 800 crore mark worldwide. It has also outpaced Allu Arjun's Pushpa 2: The Role in the Hindi market. Check the latest box office collection report here.

Moana trailer unveils Dwayne Johnson's live-action Maui. Check out release date Disney has released the live-action Moana trailer, introducing Dwayne Johnson as Maui. Here's what the first look reveals about the film.

Box office ka baap: Rajinikanth hails Aditya Dhar after Dhurandhar 2 success Rajinikanth's praise for Dhurandhar 2 is making headlines as the film dominates the box office. Here's what he said about Aditya Dhar.

Iran premieres Night Light as response to Israeli show Tehran amid war Iran's new series Night Light is drawing comparisons with Tehran, but with a different perspective. Here's why its launch is making headlines.

AI reimagines Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2 ending, fans react With Dhurandhar: The Revenge witnessing strong box office numbers, the film's emotional climax has gone viral, with fans using AI to reimagine Jaskirat's ending.

Dhurandhar 2 pushes Project Hail Mary India release, director reacts: How dare you? Project Hail Mary co-directors Phil Lord and Christopher Miller reacted to the sci-fi film's India release being rescheduled. The film was earlier expected to clash with Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge.

Drishyam 3 delayed as Georgekutty plans comeback on Mohanlal's birthday The highly anticipated Malayalam film Drishyam 3 has a new release date and is now set to arrive on Mohanlal's birthday. Here's what the delay means and why fans are excited.

Thaai Kizhavi box office: Radhika Sarathkumar film crosses Rs 75 crore mark Thaai Kizhavi crosses Rs 75 crore worldwide in 25 days, as Radhika Sarathkumar's film wins praise for blending humour with meaningful social themes

Ram Charan's Peddi update: Mrunal Thakur to perform special song, says report Amid ongoing speculation over the casting for a key song in Peddi, a report says Mrunal Thakur has bagged the opportunity.

Actor Sonu Sood on Dhurandhar 2: Great cinema doesn't just thrill Actor Sonu Sood shares his take on Dhurandhar: The Revenge as the film continues its strong box office run. Here's what he said that has caught attention.

Rula diya: Dhurandhar 2 actor Danish Pandor emotional after Aahana Kumra's praise Aahana Kumra praised Danish Pandor's performance in Dhurandhar: The Revenge. In an emotional post, she expressed pride over Danish's "magical" success journey.

Being Bade Sahab: Dhurandhar 2 actor Danish Iqbal went 9 hours in makeup Danish Iqbal shared he spent up to nine hours daily in prosthetic makeup to transform into 'Bade Sahab' in Dhurandhar: The Revenge.

general news.headline: You said, 'Let's do it': Trump shifts Iran war blame to Pete HegsethAs the Iran war drags on, Trump appears to rewrite how it began, pointing at his defence chief. But warnings, denials and shifting claims suggest a story far messier than he lets on.

$580 million bets before Trump's Iran remark. Coincidence or insider trading?Perfectly timed oil and equity trades worth $580 million before US President Donald Trump claimed "productive talks" with Iran and paused strikes, later denied by Tehran, are raising insider trading concerns.

Saudi, UAE on cusp of joining Iran fight as attacks mount, patience runs outIran's actions in the Strait of Hormuz have stoked fears of long-term leverage over a vital global energy artery. In response, Gulf states are weighing both military and financial pressure.

Claude can now control computer like a humanClaude can now get full access to your computer, do tasks by itself
Anthropic has launched a new feature for Claude that allows the AI to get complete access to your computer. The AI can then complete tasks on your behalf while you are away. This feature comes just days after the company launched "Dispatch" which allows you to control Claude via your phone.

Memes, missiles and mockery: Iran is winning the battle of perceptions against USIran isn't just fighting the US and Israel just with missiles and drones. It is flooding the internet with memes and AI-generated videos, mocking President Donald Trump. There are videos featuring Trump and other leaders as Lego and Teletubbies-style characters. In one video, Iran declares itself the 'Lord of the Strait'.

Live: Israel issues 3rd missile warning after explosions reported across countryUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The Middle East conflict is in its fourth week with no let-up, even as tentative signs of de-escalation emerge alongside continued military posturing. US President Donald Trump has announced a five-day pause on planned strikes targeting Iran's power and energy infrastructure, citing 'very good and productive' talks with Iran aimed at a comprehensive resolution of hostilities. But Tehran rejected any such talks happening. Even as diplomatic efforts gather pace, concerns persist over the security of the Strait of Hormuz, prompting multiple countries to call for the safe passage of commercial vessels. Stay with IndiaToday.in for live updates on the Middle East crisis.

Air Canada crew flung 100 metres from plane when it hit truck. She survivedThrown over 100 metres, still strapped to her seat, she lived. As details emerge from the LaGuardia crash that killed two pilots, a chilling account and haunting audio raise troubling questions.

China is rewriting classrooms for the AI age; India is still chasing marksChina is beginning to train students for the demands of a changing world, with a growing focus on skills and adaptability. India, meanwhile, remains rooted in a system where marks still decide opportunity. The gap between the two is no longer theoretical, it is starting to show.
Where Hawks dare: Exploring the impossible with Mahindra Thar Roxx and Scorpio-NWe venture into the unknown with two Mahindra SUVs and redefine what exploring the impossible means.

Dhurandhar The Revenge: Box Office soars worldwide, celebs reactDhurandhar The Revenge live updates: Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar: The Revenge is on a phenomenal run at the box office – both domestic and worldwide. But the film's real success lies in sparking a conversation about India's cinematic strength. The Ranveer Singh starrer is being appreciated from all corners.

Sensex jumps 1,000 points: Why is the stock market rising today?Today's gains on Dalal Street are being driven by easing West Asia tensions, softer crude prices and strong buying in banking stocks, as investors return to beaten-down large caps after Monday's sharp sell-off.

Inside Trump-Netanyahu call that led to strike on Iran's Ayatollah Ali KhameneiBy the time of the call, Trump had already signed off on the idea of military action, according to Reuters, but had not decided "when or under what circumstances the United States would get involved."

Dhurandhar 2: Ranveer Singh's best moments amid gore are ones that let him cryRanveer Singh delivered a career-defining performance as an action hero in Dhurandhar: The Revenge. Four powerful scenes highlighted the versatile actor's unmatched emotional depth.

Islamabad emerges as possible venue for direct talks between US, IranIslamabad could host US-Iran talks soon, according to Reuters, after Trump halted strikes on Iran's energy sites. Backchannel efforts are ongoing, though Tehran continues to deny any discussions with Washington.

US must talk directly, demands Iran as Pak, Turkey and Egypt try to broker peacePresident Trump halted strikes on Iran for five days even as Pakistan, Turkey and Egypt tried to talk to both sides about a peace deal. Tehran has insisted that Washington DC needs to be a direct participant in the talks, given it began the war.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 5 crushes Pushpa 2 Hindi, Rs 800 crore worldwideRanveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge has entered the Rs 500 crore club domestically in five days, and has surpassed the Rs 800 crore mark worldwide. It has also outpaced Allu Arjun's Pushpa 2: The Role in the Hindi market. Check the latest box office collection report here.

Video captures moment Air Canada jet crashed into fire truck at New York airportAn Air Canada Express CRJ-900 collided with a fire truck at LaGuardia Airport while taxiing in bad weather, killing both pilots and injuring two officers; 72 passengers were onboard.
Vijayan vs Vijayan: Name clash spices up Kerala poll battleIf they stay after nomination scrutiny, namesakes could end up swaying tight contests in some of the Kerala constituencies.

Rinku Singh set to be appointed regional sports officer by UP governmentIPL: Kolkata Knight Riders' Rinku Singh is set to be named as the Regional Sports Officer by the Uttar Pradesh government. Rinku is being rewarded for his exceptional service with the Indian cricket team.

Live: Rajasthan Board to declare Class 10 result at 1 pmRBSE 10th Result 2026 Live Updates: RBSE Class 10 Result 2026 is set to be announced today, with over 10 lakh students eagerly waiting to check their scores online. The Rajasthan Board will release the results on official websites, making it crucial for students to stay updated.Candidates are advised to keep their roll numbers ready to quickly access their RBSE 10th result 2026 once the link is activated at rajresults.nic.in, rajeduboard.rajasthan.gov.in

Dhurandhar 2: Aditya Dhar gave Rakesh Bedi his best comic role ever, with a catchRakesh Bedi's Jameel Jamali in Dhurandhar: The Revenge steals every frame with comic expressions and quirky dialogues, providing relief amid high-octane drama and gore. The actor's job, though, runs deeper in the script.
Massive blast rocks Texas refinery, giant plumes of smoke after huge fireAn explosion at a key US refinery triggered a massive fire and prompted a shelter-in-place order in Port Arthur.

The end of the single job: Why portfolio careers are rising in IndiaAs the India Skills Report 2026 notes, India's gig workforce is expected to cross 23 million by the end of the decade, while project-based hiring has already seen a sharp rise of nearly 40 per cent . At the same time, more than 9 in 10 employees are now using AI tools in some form, pointing to a workplace that is becoming both flexible and tech-driven.

Rs 200 crore bungalow undisclosed: Congress seeks action against Kerala BJP chiefThe party cited property tax records and his previous affidavit to back its claim, urging the Election Commission to act under the Representation of the People Act. Chandrasekhar, who has declared assets exceeding Rs 93 crore, is yet to respond to the allegations.

sports.headline: Anushka Sharma earns maiden call-up as India announce squad for South Africa T20Is Anushka Sharma earned her first India call-up as the BCCI named the squad for the women's T20I series against South Africa starting April 17. Harmanpreet Kaur continued to lead the side, with Smriti Mandhana serving as her deputy.

Krunal Pandya sets sights on more IPL success with RCB: Just the beginning RCB all-rounder Krunal Pandya was instrumental in the franchise's maiden IPL triumph in 2025, delivering a string of match-winning performances. Yet, the seasoned campaigner remains hungry for more and has already set his sights on further glory.

Rinku Singh set to be appointed regional sports officer by UP government IPL: Kolkata Knight Riders' Rinku Singh is set to be named as the Regional Sports Officer by the Uttar Pradesh government. Rinku is being rewarded for his exceptional service with the Indian cricket team.

Ben Stokes breaks silence on Ashes drubbing: Hardest period of my captaincy England captain Ben Stokes has broken his silence after the team's humiliation at the hands of Australia in Ashes 2025-26. Stokes has called the series the hardest period of his captaincy journey.

MS Dhoni should be the CSK captain in IPL 2026: Aakash Chopra IPL 2026: Aakash Chopra said MS Dhoni should captain CSK if he plays IPL 2026. His comments reignited debate over Dhoni's role in the team.

AIFF women's chief seeks answers after Asian Cup blunders, kit row erupts IPL 2026: Valanka Alemao demanded accountability after multiple lapses hit India's Asian Cup campaign. The controversy included ill-fitting jerseys and poor planning.

IPL 2026: KKR captain Ajinkya Rahane warns critics with rapid 58 in intra-squad clash IPL 2026: Ajinkya Rahane hit a rapid 58 in KKR's intra-squad clash. The knock came amid criticism over his captaincy ahead of IPL 2026.

Bumrah's words, painful lessons: Mayank Yadav's road to recovery for IPL 2026 From skipping recovery sessions to surgery and back, Mayank Yadav opens up on his painful road to IPL 2026 and the conversations with Bumrah that kept him going.

Cristiano Ronaldo shares positive injury update, eases World Cup 2026 fears Cristiano Ronaldo shared a positive recovery update after his injury setback. The Portugal captain is expected to be fit for the 2026 World Cup.

Fined by Pakistan board, Sahibzada Farhan wins ICC award for T20 World Cup show Opener Sahibzada Farhan became the first Pakistan cricketer to win the ICC Player of the Month award since November 2024. Farhan scored 383 runs, including two hundreds, in the T20 World Cup 2026.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is not professional: Jitesh Sharma lifts lid on prodigy IPL 2026: Jitesh Sharma says Sooryavanshi is 'not professional' off the field. The reason? He can't stop eating ice cream at night. Cricket's brightest teen is just being a teenager.

PSL loses another star as Rajasthan Royals confirm Sam Curran's replacementIPL 2026: Dasun Shanaka replaced injured Sam Curran in Rajasthan Royals' IPL 2026 squad. The Sri Lankan all-rounder joined the franchise for Rs 2 crore after ditching PSL.

IPL 2026: Delhi Capitals await Mitchell Starc's NOC from Cricket AustraliaIPL 2026: Delhi Capitals are awaiting Cricket Australia's NOC for Mitchell Starc. The franchise remains hopeful he will join before their IPL 2026 opener.

Gavaskar blasts IPL overseas stars, urges teams to drop players for last-minute exitIPL 2026: Sunil Gavaskar has called on IPL franchises to drop overseas players who pull out of tournament at the last minute, saying some are taking advantage of India's tradition of hospitality.

Hardik Pandya urged to step down as Mumbai captain ahead of new IPL seasonIPL 2026: Former India opener Kris Srikkanth has urged Hardik Pandya to relinquish the captaincy, saying Suryakumar Yadav, fresh from T20 World Cup success, should lead Mumbai Indians. Hardik has led MI since returning from Gujarat Titans in 2024.

Koneru Humpy withdraws from Candidates Tournament citing security concernsIndia's star chess player Koneru Humpy has withdrawn from the FIDE Women's Candidates Tournament. Humpy's decision comes after she lashed out at the organisers over safety concerns in warn-torn West Asia.

KL Rahul will open in IPL 2026, confirms Delhi Capitals captain Axar PatelSenior batter KL Rahul will open the innings for Delhi Capitals, revealed the team management ahead of the team's IPL opener. Last season, Delhi played with an array of opening combinations, resulting in inconsistent performances.

Armed group warns foreign players to skip PSL, says Pakistan not safe for sportsAn armed group has warned overseas players to skip the 2026 edition of Pakistan Super League, citing a volatile security situation and calling Pakistan unfit for peaceful sporting activity.

Dhoni shares wholesome moment with Sarfaraz Khan's son, video goes viralA heartwarming video of MS Dhoni and Sarfaraz Khan's son has gone viral. In the video, the CSK legend can be seen playing with Sarfaraz's one-year-old son.

New Zealand Cricket director resigns after NZ20 replaces iconic Super SmashNew Zealand Cricket has backed a new NZ20 franchise league to replace the Super Smash. Hours after the decision was made official, board director Dion Nash issued his resignation.

Virat Kohli hits rapid 29, Tim David, Venkatesh Iyer bat well in RCB practiceRoyal Challengers Bengaluru held their first practice game on Sunday, March 22, ahead of the Indian Premier League 2026 season. Their batters came off the blocks quickly and batted with aggressive intent.

Petrol Shortage League: Fans troll PSL over oil crisis stepsPakistan Super League is being trolled by cricket fans after the league decided to trim down its operations. The reaction comes after PCB head Mohsin Naqvi announced that the league will held in only 2 cities this season. That too, behind closed doors.
Messi scores 901st career goal as Inter Miami rally past New YorkLionel Messi scored his 901st career goal as Inter Miami came from behind to beat New York City FC 3-2 in a thrilling MLS clash.

Real Madrid beat Atletico in pulsating derby, stay alive in LaLiga raceReal Madrid vs Atletico Madrid: Vinicius Jr scored twice as Real Madrid came from behind to beat Atletico Madrid 3-2 in a thrilling derby, keeping their LaLiga title push firmly on track.

Carlos Alcaraz stunned by world No. 36 Sebastian Korda in Miami OpenMiami Open: World no.1 Carlos Alcaraz has been stunned by United States Sebastian Korda in the third round of the Miami Open. Korda, ranked 36th in the world, bested Alcaraz in three sets.

Hardik Pandya's MI goals in 2026: Win 6th title, build legacyMumbai Indians captain Hardik Pandya wants to win the 6th Indian Premier League title for the franchise in IPL 2026. In an interview, Hardik said that it was important to him that MI played their best-ever cricket this season.

PSL's loss is IPL's gain: How many players have quit Pakistan league so far?The PSL's overlap with the IPL over the last two seasons has created a growing problem for the PCB, with players increasingly leaving the Pakistan Super League for lucrative IPL replacement deals.

Arsenal's ship springs first hole as Man City snatch Carabao Cup from ArtetaArsenal's push for a historic season hit its first roadblock as Manchester City punished their mistakes in the Carabao Cup final. With bigger prizes still in sight, Arteta's side must respond quickly to avoid this becoming a familiar pattern again.

How World Cup win changed women's cricket in India: Jemimah Rodrigues explainsIn an interview with Sports Today, Jemimah Rodrigues said India's 2025 Women's World Cup win has inspired a new generation of young cricketers and boosted recognition for female players.

Suresh Raina, Matthew Hayden inducted into CSK's first-ever Hall of FameIPL 2026: Chennai Super Kings have inducted legends Suresh Raina and Matthew Hayden into their first-ever Hall of Fame, honouring their outstanding contributions to the franchise.

Virat Kohli reminisces RCB's deserving IPL 2025 triumph: It was not a flukeIPL 2026: Virat Kohli said RCB's IPL 2025 title win was no fluke. He highlighted the team's confidence and consistency throughout the campaign.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
